result.summary: Found 1 HTTP API (Webflow Data API), 3 MCP servers (main, beta, and documentation), and 1 CLI; auth uses Webflow OAuth, site tokens, and workspace tokens.

wf_oauth_app.label: Webflow OAuth app
wf_oauth_app.setup: Create/register an app in Webflow’s developer platform from the OAuth docs at [OAuth](https://developers.webflow.com/data/reference/oauth-app). Use the app’s client credentials in the standard Webflow OAuth flow to obtain bearer access to user-authorized sites/workspaces for the Data API and MCP server.

wf_site_token.label: Webflow Site API token
wf_site_token.setup: Generate a site-scoped token from your Webflow site settings as described in [Site Token](https://developers.webflow.com/data/reference/authentication/site-token). The CLI can also acquire and store a site token by running `webflow auth login`, which writes `WEBFLOW_SITE_ID` and `WEBFLOW_SITE_API_TOKEN` to your project `.env` file per [CLI authentication](https://developers.webflow.com/cli/authentication).

wf_workspace_token.label: Webflow Workspace API token
wf_workspace_token.setup: Generate a workspace-scoped token from your Webflow workspace settings as described in [Workspace Token](https://developers.webflow.com/data/reference/authentication/workspace-token). For Code Components/DevLink workflows, the CLI can obtain and store one by running `webflow devlink import` (browser auth) or you can pass it directly with `--api-token` in CI/CD per [CLI authentication](https://developers.webflow.com/cli/authentication).
